sources.title: Why Service is the Largest Single Influence on the Public's Perception of Your Dealership
sources.excerpt: NADA frames fixed ops as a core profit and perception driver, and cites phone, voicemail, and transfer breakdowns as a source of roughly $1M in annual lost dealership revenue.
sources.relevance_reason: Supports the article's core service-drive capacity argument and the NADA-cited $1M leakage claim.

sources.title: Adopting Artificial Intelligence in the Service Department
sources.excerpt: CDK Global discusses AI adoption in dealership service departments and the operational role of automation in fixed operations.
sources.relevance_reason: Supports the section explaining why service departments need systems that absorb routine demand rather than relying only on advisor effort.
sources.freshness_override_reason: Older than the default 24-month window, but used as evergreen fixed-ops operational context from a recognized dealership technology source.

sources.title: Is Your Service Department in Tune With Customer Expectations?
sources.excerpt: CDK Global covers how dealership service communication and customer expectations affect retention and service experience.
sources.relevance_reason: Supports the follow-up and communication sections, especially the idea that service retention depends on timely, relationship-aware updates.
sources.freshness_override_reason: Older than the default 24-month window, but relevant as evergreen service-customer expectation context and materially updated in 2025.

sources.title: Turn Service Visits into Sales Growth: A GM's Checklist
sources.excerpt: automotiveMastermind frames the service drive as a growth channel and recommends identifying sales opportunities while customers are already in service.
sources.relevance_reason: Supports the triage argument that service visits should surface retention and sales opportunities before they disappear.

sources.notes: Four usable non-competitor sources are present. Two older CDK sources include freshness overrides.

competitor.notes: No blocked competitor names or links appear in publish output.

content_quality.notes: The article stays dealership-specific, explains the service capacity leak clearly, and includes operational audit steps.

seo_aeo.notes: Clean title, meta description, slug, direct answer opening, structured headings, FAQ, and entity-rich service-drive language.

links.notes: Four external source links and four internal links are present, with descriptive anchors.

calculator.notes: No calculator is required for this article because the post is an operational diagnostic, not a numeric estimation page.

visual.notes: Featured image is configured and article hero is intentionally hidden. Blog hub image behavior was fixed separately in production.
